seoDescription: Audience guides: funding and first dev, solo dev, PM/PO, phased rebuild, editorial platform, SaaS MVP, product AI. Practical advice and how I can help.
title: Who it's for, and how I can help
lede: Every context has its traps. These pages cover common situations — and why fractional or phased senior support can beat a rushed hire or a « we'll deal with it later ».

premier-dev-fractionnel.title: After your first raise: before hiring a full-time developer
premier-dev-fractionnel.teaser: Why two days a week with an experienced Product Engineer can deliver more value than a first junior hire — within the same budget band.

dev-unique-backup.title: One developer owns all the tech
dev-unique-backup.teaser: How one day a week reduces bus factor, adds day-to-day value, and covers you when your main developer is on vacation.

product-manager-fractionnel.title: You're looking for a Product Manager or Product Owner
product-manager-fractionnel.teaser: Real PM need without full-time: about four days per week as half-days, full days to prototype and validate, prioritization and dev ceremonies.

refonte-produit-par-phases.title: Product rebuild or stack change
refonte-produit-par-phases.teaser: Feasibility, phasing, and architecture without big bang — without becoming tech lead or running your devs day to day.

plateforme-editoriale-produit.title: Editorial product at scale (comparator, multi-brand)
plateforme-editoriale-produit.teaser: Content and business-journey product — not a brochure site: blocks, APIs, headless when relevant, fewer ad-hoc integrations per launch.

mvp-saas-faisabilite.title: Launching a SaaS or MVP
mvp-saas-faisabilite.teaser: Phased framing before big spend: feasibility, stack, prototypes, and prerequisites for your agency or devs.

ia-produit-garde-fous.title: AI in your product
ia-produit-garde-fous.teaser: Where AI fits, guardrails, and POC before build — product and technical decisions, not hype or black box.
